mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero constrains monetary policy transmission and signals exhausted easing capacity. Under this regime, equity valuations become disconnected from traditional discount rate mechanics, and forward returns depend on real economic growth rather than rate expectations. Current FEDFUNDS=0.1% indicates this constraint is binding.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and GDPC1 growth is below trend, or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window contradicts negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. A 0.3 percentage point or greater increase in UNRATE over a 3-month window predicts negative equity returns as earnings expectations decline and recession risk rises.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE increase, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth rate directly reflects consumer demand strength and corporate earnings visibility. Sustained month-over-month RSAFS growth above 0.5% signals robust demand and predicts positive SPX returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions flow through equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong RSAFS growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization and supply-side health. When INDPRO declines below its 12-month moving average, it signals supply constraints or demand weakness that compresses profit margins. This predicts negative 5-day returns as market reprices earnings downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances despite INDPRO below trend, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal broad-based economic momentum. When both series show positive 3-month growth rates simultaneously, it indicates synchronized demand expansion across residential and labor markets, predicting positive 20-day SPX returns through improved earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST=699.0 thousand units, PAYEMS=132697.0 thousand persons
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when both HOUST and PAYEMS show positive 3-month growth, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and monetary policy stance (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real interest rates. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS rate, real rates are negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations. This mechanism predicts positive 60-day returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when 12-month CPI growth exceeds FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) is an always-on trend signal. Positive month-over-month PAYEMS growth consistently predicts positive 20-day SPX returns through improved consumer confidence and corporate earnings visibility. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 58% of months with positive PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When both decline simultaneously over 3 months, it signals a supply shock or demand collapse that predicts negative 5-day SPX returns as profit margins compress and recession risk rises.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 62% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
